cards.front: What does (8 hours) signify in the context of optimization technique?
cards.back: The amount of time allocated for the optimization technique

cards.front: What is the time limit for applying the (8 hours) optimization technique?
cards.back: Eight consecutive hours, usually during an internship or a specific period

cards.front: What is the main objective of the (8 hours) optimization technique?
cards.back: To gain practical experience and apply theoretical knowledge to real-world problems

cards.front: Who is credited with introducing the (8 hours) optimization technique?
cards.back: A.P. Mathew and Harvey Mudd College are credited with introducing the (8 hours) approach

cards.front: What is the primary benefit of the (8 hours) optimization technique?
cards.back: It provides students with hands-on experience and exposure to real-world problems
